item.description: The Justice Department announced that President Trump would be dropping his lawsuit against the Internal Revenue Service in "exchange" for the creation of an "anti-weaponization fund." NBC News' Monica Alba reports on the announcement and how the fund could be used.

media:content.media:title: Justice Department sets up 'anti-weaponization fund' after Trump drops IRS lawsuit
media:content.media:description: The Justice Department announced that President Trump would be dropping his lawsuit against the Internal Revenue Service in "exchange" for the creation of an "anti-weaponization fund." NBC News' Monica Alba reports on the announcement and how the fund could be used.

item.title: Justice Department sets up 'anti-weaponization fund' after Trump drops IRS lawsuit

item.description: It is a high stakes political week for President Donald Trump with primary elections that could indicate the future of the Republican party. A key test for Trump’s power is coming up in Kentucky. The Iran war has become a central political issue for Trump and Democrats argue voters will express their frustration at the ballot box. NBC’s Ryan Nobles reports for TODAY.

media:content.media:title: High-Stakes Primary Elections Test Trump’s Influence of GOP
media:content.media:description: It is a high stakes political week for President Donald Trump with primary elections that could indicate the future of the Republican party. A key test for Trump’s power is coming up in Kentucky. The Iran war has become a central political issue for Trump and Democrats argue voters will express their frustration at the ballot box. NBC’s Ryan Nobles reports for TODAY.

item.title: High-Stakes Primary Elections Test Trump’s Influence of GOP

item.description: The CDC confirmed one American who was working in Congo tested positive for Ebola after becoming symptomatic. A CDC spokesman said the person and other high-risk contacts will be moved to Germany for treatment and monitoring. NBC News medical reporter Dr. Akshay Syal has details on the announcement.

media:content.media:title: CDC confirms one American working in Congo tested positive for Ebola
media:content.media:description: The CDC confirmed one American who was working in Congo tested positive for Ebola after becoming symptomatic. A CDC spokesman said the person and other high-risk contacts will be moved to Germany for treatment and monitoring. NBC News medical reporter Dr. Akshay Syal has details on the announcement.

item.title: CDC confirms one American working in Congo tested positive for Ebola

item.dc:creator: Chantal Da Silva
item.description: President Donald Trump said Monday that he is calling off an attack on Iran planned for Tuesday because regional leaders had urged him to allow negotiations to continue and that a “very acceptable” deal for the U.S. was at hand

media:content.media:text: TOPSHOT-IRAN-US-ISRAEL-WAR-PROTEST
media:content.media:description: Women hold Iran's national flags during an anti-US and Israel protest at the Hafte Tir Square in Tehran on May 17, 2026.

media:content.media:text: Iran US
media:content.media:description: The Barakah nuclear power plant in Abu Dhabi's western desert.

media:content.media:text: Iran And US Remain In Stalemate Over Strait Of Hormuz
media:content.media:description: A ship remains anchored in the Strait of Hormuz near Larak Island, Iran, on Saturday.

item.title: Trump says an attack on Iran planned for Tuesday is called off

item.dc:creator: Daniella Silva
item.description: An ICE agent who shot a Venezuelan man and is accused by Minnesota prosecutors of lying about the incident was charged with assault and falsely reporting a crime in Minnesota on Monday.

media:content.media:text: Image: Law enforcement officers stand amid tear gas at the scene of a reported shooting in Minneapolis
media:content.media:description: Law enforcement officers stand amid tear gas at the scene of a reported shooting in Minneapolis, Jan. 14, 2026.

item.title: ICE agent charged with four counts of assault in Minnesota shooting of Venezuelan immigrant

item.dc:creator: Marlene Lenthang
item.description: Vendors in and around SoFi Stadium in Inglewood, Calif., have expressed fear as the Trump administration has signaled ICE will help provide security for the World Cup.

media:content.media:text: Henrry Josue uses tongs to grab hotdogs on a tray outside
media:content.media:description: Henrry Josue, a 23-year-old vendor, sells hot dogs outside a Charlie Puth concert at the Kia Forum in Inglewood, Calif., on April 29.

media:content.media:text: A aerial view of a truck with a banner on top that reads "ICE out of LA", driving next to a large group protestors walking down the street
media:content.media:description: Henrry Josue, a 23-year-old vendor, sells hot dogs outside a Charlie Puth concert at the Kia Forum in Inglewood, Calif., on April 29.

media:content.media:text: A person holds a Charlie Puth t-shirt in a parking lot
media:content.media:description: Henrry Josue, a 23-year-old vendor, sells hot dogs outside a Charlie Puth concert at the Kia Forum in Inglewood, Calif., on April 29.

media:content.media:text: A split composite image of vendors walking carts across a street between cars, left, and a vendor selling food to a patron
media:content.media:description: Henrry Josue, a 23-year-old vendor, sells hot dogs outside a Charlie Puth concert at the Kia Forum in Inglewood, Calif., on April 29.

media:content.media:text: Protestors stand in a line holding signs that read "Kick ICE Out! Unite Here! Local 11"
media:content.media:description: Henrry Josue, a 23-year-old vendor, sells hot dogs outside a Charlie Puth concert at the Kia Forum in Inglewood, Calif., on April 29.

media:content.media:text: People stand near vendor carts in a parking lot outside of the Kia Forum building
media:content.media:description: Henrry Josue, a 23-year-old vendor, sells hot dogs outside a Charlie Puth concert at the Kia Forum in Inglewood, Calif., on April 29.

media:content.media:text: Two women walk outside rolling food carts beside them in a parking lot next to a street, their backs are to camera
media:content.media:description: Henrry Josue, a 23-year-old vendor, sells hot dogs outside a Charlie Puth concert at the Kia Forum in Inglewood, Calif., on April 29.

item.title: Amid prospect of ICE at World Cup games, vendors weigh livelihoods against safety

item.dc:creator: Nicole Acevedo
item.description: In Mexico, the girl’s condition has worsened and she’s not getting the specialized care she needs, her family said in an exclusive interview with NBC News.

media:content.media:text: A 10-year-old girl recovering from brain cancer, from the United States was deported with her undocumented parents last month.
media:content.media:description: A 12-year-old girl who is a U.S. citizen and is recovering from a brain tumor went with her undocumented parents to Mexico when they were deported last February. Her face has been blurred for her safety.

media:content.media:text: Immigration authorities removed four American citizen children from Texas, including a 10-year-old girl recovering from brain cancer, from the United States when they deported their undocumented parents last month.
media:content.media:description: A 12-year-old girl who is a U.S. citizen and is recovering from a brain tumor went with her undocumented parents to Mexico when they were deported last February. Her face has been blurred for her safety.

item.title: Humanitarian parole denied for deported parents of U.S. citizen girl with rare brain tumor

item.description: A jury in California tossed Elon Musk's lawsuit against OpenAI and CEO Sam Altman after determining Musk waited too long to bring the lawsuit forward.

media:content.media:title: Jury tosses Elon Musk's lawsuit against OpenAI and Sam Altman
media:content.media:description: A jury in California tossed Elon Musk's lawsuit against OpenAI and CEO Sam Altman after determining Musk waited too long to bring the lawsuit forward.

item.title: Jury tosses Elon Musk's lawsuit against OpenAI and Sam Altman

item.dc:creator: Nicole Acevedo
Pilar Melendez
Juliette Arcodia
item.description: South Carolina’s Supreme Court recently overturned Murdaugh’s double murder convictions, ruling that Colleton County Clerk of Court Becky Hill had improperly influenced the jury.

media:content.media:text: murdaugh in court orange prison clothing
media:content.media:description: Alex Murdaugh during his sentencing in Beaufort, S.C., on Nov. 28, 2023.

item.title: Alex Murdaugh files civil rights suit against ex-court clerk over alleged jury tampering

item.dc:creator: Rohan Nadkarni
Greg Rosenstein
item.description: The NBA’s Conference Finals kick off Monday with a matchup between the San Antonio Spurs and Oklahoma City Thunder, while the East gets going tomorrow with the New York Knicks hosting the Cleveland Cavaliers.

media:content.media:text: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander of the Oklahoma City Thunder drives to the basket during a game against the Los Angeles Lakers on May 11, 2026.
media:content.media:description: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander of the Oklahoma City Thunder drives to the basket during a game against the Los Angeles Lakers on May 11, 2026.

media:content.media:text: Donovan Mitchell reacts during the fourth quarter against the Detroit Pistons in Game Seven of the Second Round of the NBA Eastern Conference Playoffs.
media:content.media:description: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander of the Oklahoma City Thunder drives to the basket during a game against the Los Angeles Lakers on May 11, 2026.

media:content.media:text: Luis Diaz of Bayern Munich controls the ball.
media:content.media:description: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander of the Oklahoma City Thunder drives to the basket during a game against the Los Angeles Lakers on May 11, 2026.

item.title: From The Sports Desk: The NBA’s Final Four is here

item.dc:creator: Francie Ebert
item.description: Kars4Kids must stop broadcasting its widely known catchy jingle in advertisements across California after a judge found that the nonprofit violated the state’s false advertising and unfair competition laws

media:content.media:text: Kars4Kids TV commercial.
media:content.media:description: Kars4Kids TV commercial.

item.title: Judge bans Kars4Kids jingle in California, citing ‘misleading’ advertising

item.dc:creator: Daniella Silva
item.description: An ICE agent who shot a Venezuelan man and is accused by Minnesota prosecutors of lying about the incident was charged with assault and falsely reporting a crime in Minnesota on Monday.

media:content.media:text: Image: Law enforcement officers stand amid tear gas at the scene of a reported shooting in Minneapolis
media:content.media:description: Law enforcement officers stand amid tear gas at the scene of a reported shooting in Minneapolis, Jan. 14, 2026.

item.title: ICE agent charged with four counts of assault in Minnesota shooting of Venezuelan immigrant

item.dc:creator: Aria Bendix
item.description: A person from the U.S. has tested positive for Ebola in connection with the deadly outbreak in Congo and Uganda, the CDC announced Monday.

media:content.media:text: A visitor washes his hands at a sink operated by a hospital worker before entering Kyeshero Hospital
media:content.media:description: A visitor washes his hands before entering Kyeshero Hospital at a checkpoint for hand washing and temperature screening for all visitors and patients entering Kyeshero Hospital, as part of Ebola prevention measures, in Goma , Democratic Republic of Congo, on May 18, 2026.

item.title: American working in Congo tests positive for Ebola, CDC says

item.dc:creator: Rebecca Shabad
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth on Monday spoke at a campaign event boosting the President Donald Trump-backed challenger to Rep.

media:content.media:text: Pete Hegseth stands near an American flag behind him
media:content.media:description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth during a news conference at the Pentagon in Arlington, Va., on April 8, 2026.

media:content.media:text: President Trump Visits A Packaging Facility In Kentucky
media:content.media:description: President Donald Trump shakes hands with Republican congressional candidate for Kentucky, Ed Gallrein as they speak on stage at Verst Logistics on March 11 in Hebron, Ky.

item.title: Pete Hegseth boosts Trump-backed challenger to Rep. Thomas Massie ahead of Kentucky primary

item.dc:creator: Corky Siemaszko
item.description: It was manic Monday for thousands of Long Island Rail Road riders trying to get in and out of New York City on the third day of a labor strike that has shut down the nation’s largest commuter railroad

media:content.media:text: Long Island Rail Road (LIRR) workers picket in front of Penn Station in New York City
media:content.media:description: Long Island Rail Road workers picket in front of Penn Station in New York, on Sunday.

item.title: Long Island Rail Road strike creates manic Monday commute as negotiators return to the table

item.dc:creator: David Ingram
item.description: The jury ruled that Elon Musk had waited too long to sue OpenAI and its other co-founders.

media:content.media:text: Photos of Elon Musk and Sam Altman in elevators at federal court in Oakland, Calif.
media:content.media:description: Elon Musk and Sam Altman in elevators at federal court in Oakland, Calif.

item.title: Jury throws out Elon Musk’s lawsuit against OpenAI and Sam Altman in less than two hours

item.dc:creator: Nicole Acevedo
Pilar Melendez
Juliette Arcodia
item.description: South Carolina’s Supreme Court recently overturned Murdaugh’s double murder convictions, ruling that Colleton County Clerk of Court Becky Hill had improperly influenced the jury.

media:content.media:text: murdaugh in court orange prison clothing
media:content.media:description: Alex Murdaugh during his sentencing in Beaufort, S.C., on Nov. 28, 2023.

item.title: Alex Murdaugh files civil rights suit against ex-court clerk over alleged jury tampering

item.dc:creator: Ariana Brockington, TODAY
item.description: Barnes & Noble CEO James Daunt has learned to adapt to changes in the book publishing industry to keep his company afloat and thriving.

media:content.media:text: Barnes & Noble CEO James Daunt.
media:content.media:description: Barnes & Noble CEO James Daunt.

item.title: Why the CEO of Barnes & Noble would support selling AI-written books in stores

item.dc:creator: Matt Dixon
item.description: A group that says its mission is to serve as a prediction market “watchdog” is launching a six-figure ad buy this week in the Washington, D.C., media market.

media:content.media:text: Kalshi advertisement of a passing truck
media:content.media:description: Prediction markets won't have any advertising spots during the Super Bowl broadcast. But this truck, hyping up the action on Kalshi, was spotted in San Francisco days ahead of the big game in Santa Clara.

item.title: Prediction market ‘watchdog’ launches six-figure ad campaign ahead of Senate hearing

item.dc:creator: Rebecca Cohen
item.description: Two people were arrested in Japan after a person broke into the viral macaque Punch’s enclosure at a zoo outside of Tokyo, officials said

media:content.media:text: A macaque holds a stuffed toy monkey while walking around an enclosure
media:content.media:description: Punch, a baby macaque, who resides at Ichikawa City Zoo in Chiba, Japan.

item.title: 2 arrested after person breaks into Punch the monkey enclosure in Japan, zoo says

item.dc:creator: David K. Li
item.description: Actor Russell Andrews, whose credits include “Better Call Saul,” “Grey’s Anatomy” and “Insecure,” said over the weekend that he has been diagnosed with ALS.

media:content.media:text: Actor Russell Andrews on CNN's "The Story Is with Elex Michaelson".
media:content.media:description: Russell Andrews on CNN's "The Story Is with Elex Michaelson."

item.title: Actor Russell Andrews reveals ALS diagnosis

item.dc:creator: Marlene Lenthang

item.dc:creator: Adam Reiss
Daniel Arkin
item.description: The judge overseeing the state murder trial of Luigi Mangione, the man accused of gunning down UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson, ruled Monday that prosecutors can use as evidence a gun and a notebook previously described as a “manifesto.”Judge Gregory Carro’s ruling effectively rejected the defense team’s argument that those items were seized illegally, delivering a partial victory to prosecutors

media:content.media:text: Luigi Mangione.
media:content.media:description: Luigi Mangione appears in Manhattan Supreme Court in New York, on Dec. 18, 2025.

item.title: Luigi Mangione prosecutors can use gun and notebook as evidence, judge rules

item.dc:creator: Ryan J. Reilly
Gary Grumbach
Megan Lebowitz
item.description: The Justice Department on Monday announced that it was establishing a $1.776 billion “Anti-Weaponization Fund” after President Donald Trump moved to dismiss a $10 billion lawsuit against the IRS over his leaked tax returns

media:content.media:text: President Trump Returns To White House From China
media:content.media:description: President Donald Trump speaks to members of the media on the South Lawn of the White House after arriving on Marine One on Friday, May 15, 2026.

item.title: DOJ sets up $1.8B ‘anti-weaponization’ fund after Trump drops IRS lawsuit

item.dc:creator: Jennifer Jett
item.description: The stakes were high and the deals were modest, but President Donald Trump may have left China last week having laid solid groundwork with Chinese leader Xi Jinping — though a number of issues could still sour ties.

media:content.media:text: TOPSHOT-CHINA-US-DIPLOMACY
media:content.media:description: TOPSHOT - US President Donald Trump (R) shakes hands with China's President Xi Jinping at the Great Hall of the People in Beijing on May 14, 2026.

media:content.media:text: U.S. President Trump Meets With China's President Xi And Attends State Banquet
media:content.media:description: Trump and Xi greet officials at the Great Hall of the People in Beijing.

item.title: A trade boost and a new relationship status: U.S. touts modest China deals after summit’s good vibes
